purpose: Route a source document to relevant binary SpecificTopic family KMs. This router does not decide tags — it selects which family KMs to run.
routing_policy: Choose families from source-supported title/scope/definitions/operative provisions — not filenames, URLs, or row IDs.
Default to 1-2 families for narrow sources; add more only for separate central legal pillars.
The router must never output final SpecificTopic labels.
When two families could both apply, use the disambiguation_rules below to pick the primary one.

pollution_chemicals_air_soil is for regulations primarily about chemical substances, industrial air/soil emissions, or contaminated land — NOT for regulations where water bodies, marine environment, or wastewater treatment are the central subject.
If the document's primary subject is water quality, wastewater, river basins, marine strategy, groundwater, or fisheries — always prefer water_marine_fisheries over pollution_chemicals_air_soil.

If the document's primary subject is product liability, consumer protection, GMO traceability, product safety, or data privacy — always prefer consumers_products_privacy, even if chemicals or substances are mentioned in a product context.

disambiguation_rules.when: Title or scope primarily concerns wastewater treatment, water quality standards, river basin management, groundwater, drinking water, bathing water, marine environment, marine strategy, or fisheries
Title or scope primarily concerns defective products, product liability, consumer protection law, GMO traceability/labelling, product safety, data protection, or GDPR
Title or scope primarily concerns end-of-life vehicles, WEEE, batteries, packaging waste, or circular economy
Title or scope primarily concerns habitats, biodiversity, Natura 2000, species conservation, invasive species, or wildlife trade
Title or scope primarily concerns fundamental rights, anti-corruption, non-financial reporting, ESG disclosure, or corporate governance

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Climate, Carbon, Energy and Buildings
run_when: The source mentions climate change, net zero, greenhouse gases, NDCs, emissions reductions, carbon pricing, carbon markets, ETS, allowances or credits.
The source regulates renewable energy, energy efficiency, energy labelling, building energy performance, CCS, or energy audits.
The source regulates vehicle, fuel, installation, building, or product emissions as climate/energy mechanisms.
do_not_run_when: The source only mentions emissions incidentally as part of product safety, wastewater, or chemical classification rules — without climate/energy targets or mechanisms.
owned_tags_after_deduplication: Carbon Trading and Emissions Trading Schemes

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Pollution, Chemicals, Air and Soil
run_when: The source primarily regulates air quality standards, industrial air emissions, combustion plant emissions, ambient air pollutants, transboundary air pollution, or ozone-depleting substances.
The source primarily regulates chemical substances, hazardous chemicals, POPs, REACH, biocides, pesticides, asbestos, silica, or radioactive waste.
The source primarily regulates soil contamination, contaminated land remediation, or soil quality standards.
The source primarily regulates releases of chemical substances or industrial pollutants — where chemicals or air/soil emissions are the CENTRAL operative subject, not incidental.
do_not_run_when: The primary subject is water bodies, wastewater treatment, river basins, groundwater, drinking water, marine environment, or fisheries — use water_marine_fisheries instead.
The primary subject is product liability, consumer product safety, defective products, GMO traceability, or data privacy — use consumers_products_privacy instead.
The primary subject is end-of-life vehicles, batteries, WEEE, or circular economy — use waste_circular_products instead.

Water pollution is mentioned only incidentally as a context for a regulation whose core subject is something else (e.g. Nitrates Directive primarily protects water quality → route to water_marine_fisheries).

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Water, Marine Resources and Fisheries
run_when: The source primarily regulates wastewater treatment, sewage treatment, or urban waste-water collection and discharge.
The source primarily regulates water quality standards, river basin management, groundwater protection, drinking water quality, or bathing water quality.
The source primarily regulates the marine environment, marine strategy, coastal zones, marine protected areas, or ocean quality.
The source primarily regulates fisheries, fish stocks, marine living resources, or aquaculture.
The source regulates nitrate or agricultural runoff where the PRIMARY protected object is water body quality.
Run here even if the regulation mentions pollution — if water bodies, marine areas, or wastewater are what the regulation is fundamentally protecting or managing.

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Waste, Circular Economy and Product Stewardship
run_when: The source mentions waste, recycling, reuse, recovery, packaging, WEEE, batteries, product stewardship, producer responsibility, circular economy, resource efficiency, end-of-life products, or electrical/electronic equipment.

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Biodiversity, Ecosystems, Land and Animal Welfare
run_when: The source centrally regulates biodiversity, habitats, species, Natura 2000/protected areas, ecosystem restoration, invasive species, wildlife trade, pollinators, forestry/nature protection, soil conservation, animal welfare, or land-use planning/EIA/SEA as the operative mechanism.

For EIA/SEA/planning laws, route here primarily for Land Use Planning; biodiversity/ecosystem labels require explicit biodiversity, species, habitats, protected-area, ecosystem, or nature-protection operative duties.
owned_tags_after_deduplication: Direct Impact Drivers of Biodiversity Loss
Impacts on the State of Species
Impacts on the Extent and Conditions of Ecosystems
Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Own Workforce, Labor Conditions and Worker Rights
run_when: The source mentions employees, workers, employment contracts, working conditions, wages, minimum wage, working time, leave, workplace safety, discrimination, equal pay, collective bargaining, social dialogue, forced labor, child labor, trafficking, training, disability inclusion, or harassment.
owned_tags_after_deduplication: Measures Against Violence and Harassment in the Workplace

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Affected Communities and Indigenous Rights
run_when: Run rarely, only when affected communities, Indigenous peoples, community rights, resettlement, housing rights, community consultation/consent, cultural/community rights, or explicit community-facing rights mechanisms are central.

Do not run for ordinary public participation, public notice, EIA/SEA consultation, land-use planning, biodiversity, or climate adaptation unless affected communities or community rights are explicit central objects.
owned_tags_after_deduplication: Communities' Economic, Social, and Cultural Rights

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Consumers, End-Users, Product Safety and Privacy
run_when: The source primarily regulates defective products, product liability, consumer protection law, or product safety standards.
The source regulates GMO traceability, GMO labelling, or genetically modified organism approvals.

The source mentions consumers, end-users, product safety, labels, product information, recalls, marketing practices, unfair commercial practices, data protection, GDPR, personal data, privacy, biocidal products, food information, toys, cosmetics, textiles, or product certification.

Also run for hybrid emissions/chemical/product laws when product safety, product information, consumer protection, privacy, market placement, safe use, data sharing, or product-risk controls are title/scope/operative objects.
do_not_run_when: The regulation primarily concerns industrial chemical substance management (REACH, POPs, biocide active substances) without a direct consumer-facing product placement or safety angle — use pollution_chemicals_air_soil instead.
owned_tags_after_deduplication: Information-Related Impacts on Consumers and End-Users
Personal Safety of Non-Consumers and End-Users
Social Inclusion of Consumers and End-Users

families.name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Governance, Reporting, Due Diligence and Business Conduct
run_when: The source centrally regulates anti-corruption, bribery, AML/terrorism financing, gifts/hospitality, lobbying, political engagement, whistleblowing/protected disclosures, corporate culture, business conduct, supplier payment practices, or CSR/responsible-business conduct.
The source centrally establishes ESG/sustainability/non-financial/financial reporting or disclosure architecture, not merely operational reporting to a regulator.

The source centrally regulates due diligence, supply-chain/value-chain adverse-impact processes, compliance-management systems, legal conformity programs, certification/compliance status, regulated registers, approval/ratification regimes, authorization/compliance regimes, or economic/criminal compliance offence frameworks.

Do not run for ordinary environmental/social/product legislation merely because it contains duties, permits, monitoring, competent authorities, penalties, sanctions, transposition, implementation, or enforcement language.
owned_tags_after_deduplication: Relationships with Suppliers, Including Payment Practices

topic_families_to_run.slug: one of the slug values from families[] above (e.g. water_marine_fisheries)
topic_families_to_run.reason: source-supported routing reason citing title/scope/operative provisions
topic_families_to_run.evidence_excerpt: short excerpt from the document supporting this routing decision
